speaker: Bree
content: And so there are just the four of us. My older son, Andrew, is 16, Danielle is 15 and...

speaker: Dr. Goldfine
content: I don't need to see pictures. Bree, you've spent most of the hour engaging in small talk.
mark: most of the hour 这一小时里的大部分时间
engage v. 雇用，聘请；参加，从事；吸引，引起；占用 engage in 参与，从事
engage in small talk 闲聊

speaker: Dr. Goldfine
content: Yes, Rex has been very vocal about his issues. Don't you want to discuss your feelings about your marriage?
mark: vocal adj. 直言不讳的，大声表达的；嗓音的，发声的，歌唱的
issue n. 议题，争论点；v. 发表，颁布；分发，分配
Don't you want to do sth? 你难道不想做某事吗？

speaker: Rex
content: This is the thing you got to know about Bree. She doesn't like to talk about her feelings. To be honest, it's hard to know if she has any.

speaker: Rex
content: Does she feel anger, rage, ecstasy? Who knows? She's always pleasant, and I can't tell you how annoying that is.
mark: rage n. 盛怒，狂怒；v. 发脾气，大怒；
pleasant adj. 令人愉快的，和蔼可亲的
annoying adj. 令人恼火的，令人烦躁的

speaker: Rex
content: Whatever she feels is so far below the surface, no one can see it. I feel like she's using all these domestic things..
mark: below the surface 在表面之下

speaker: Dr. Goldfine
content: Bree? Would you like to respond to what Rex just said?

speaker: Dr. Goldfine
content: Is there some truth there? Do you use housework as a way to disengage emotionally?
mark: Is there some truth there? 这是真的吗
emotionally adv. 感情上，情绪上
disengage emotionally 情感上的疏离，情绪上的释放
